summary: Large relink of the item (`it`) library. The PR reclassified several item objects in `configure.py`, removed separate `it_3F14`, `it_3F23`, and `it_3F31` link units, folded their data range into `melee/it/it_2725.c`, and adjusted `obj_files.mk` so selected item files are linked from asm rather than C. It also cleaned item/fighter forward declarations and headers, added `src/melee/it/inlines.h`, moved large header documentation into `.dox` files, and marked `item.c` and `it_26B1.c` as matching. There were no review comments or PR body text in the slice, so motivation beyond relinking/matching hygiene is inferred only from the title and diffs.
what_changed: `configure.py` removed `Object(Unknown, "melee/it/it_3F14.c")`, `it_3F23.c`, and `it_3F31.c` from the item library object list.

`configure.py` reclassified `itcapsule.c`, `itsword.c`, and `itparasol.c` from `Unknown` to `NonMatching`, while `itfoxlaser.c`, `itfoxillusion.c`, `it_2ADA.c`, and `itluigifireball.c` became `Matching`.
`obj_files.mk` removed combined src+asm entries for `it_3F14`, `it_3F23`, and `it_3F31`; `asm/melee/it/it_3F14.s`, `asm/melee/it/it_3F23.s`, and `asm/melee/it/it_3F31.s` were deleted.

`GALE01/splits.txt` style section data in the diff changed so `melee/it/it_2725.c` owns `.data start:0x803F1418 end:0x803F5468`, replacing separate data ranges for `it_3F14.c`, `it_3F23.c`, and `it_3F31.c`.
`asm/melee/it/it_2725.s` gained `.file "it_2725.c"` and a much larger assembly body/data footprint, consistent with the relinked item unit.
`obj_files.mk` switched `itcapsule`, `itparasol`, and `itsword` item object selection toward asm objects, while their C files lost large chunks of inline/ASM content.
`src/melee/it/inlines.h` was added with `GET_ITEM(gobj)` and `static inline Item* GetItemData(HSD_GObj* gobj)` for item user-data access.
`src/melee/it/item.c` added local static prototypes, made internal allocation data/static helper functions private with `static`, and included `it/inlines.h` plus `it/types.h`.
`item.h`, `it_26B1.h`, and `items/it_27CF.h` were substantially reduced, while corresponding `.dox` files were added/expanded to hold file-level documentation and todo text.
`src/melee/it/forward.h` and `src/melee/ft/forward.h` were reorganized with many explicit forward typedefs, reducing reliance on heavier type headers.
`src/melee/ft/types.h` changed `typedef struct _CollData { ... } CollData;` style into `struct CollData { ... };` paired with a forward typedef in `ft/forward.h`.
Several fighter source files gained narrower item includes, e.g. `ftCo_AirCatch.c` includes `it/inlines.h`, and `ftCo_DownAttack.c`/`ftGw_Init.c` include `it/types.h`.
smart_moves: The relink did not just edit section metadata; it aligned `configure.py`, `obj_files.mk`, asm files, and headers together so the build graph and link layout stayed coherent.
Moving large documentation blocks out of hot headers into `.dox` files reduced include surface while preserving searchable API notes and todos.
Adding `it/inlines.h` centralized the common item user-data idiom instead of duplicating casts across fighter/item code.
Making helper symbols and allocation globals in `item.c` `static` clarified file-local linkage, which is important when relinking object boundaries.
Forward-declaration cleanup in `ft/forward.h` and `it/forward.h` avoided full type includes where only pointers or typedefs were needed.
Keeping `itcapsule`, `itparasol`, and `itsword` as `NonMatching` while routing selected output through asm avoided overclaiming decomp status during a link-layout repair.
The `.file "...c"` directives in asm outputs preserve source-object identity after moving or consolidating asm, making map/debug attribution more stable.
decomp_lessons: When relinking a library, update all three layers together: object status in `configure.py`, actual object selection in `obj_files.mk`, and section ownership/split metadata.
If old data-only or split objects are collapsed into a neighboring translation unit, verify the exact `.data` address range ownership rather than only deleting files.
Headers used widely by fighter and item code should prefer forward declarations and small inline helpers over pulling in full `types.h` dependencies.
Do not keep large Doxygen/comment blocks in headers if they interfere with dependency hygiene; `.dox` sidecars can retain documentation without affecting compile/link behavior.
Mark objects `Matching` only when the relinked source object truly matches; otherwise use `NonMatching` and keep asm fallback/object routing explicit.
Static internal functions and file-local globals are useful relink tools because they prevent accidental exported symbols from changing object boundaries.
A relink PR can legitimately move code from C-with-ASM blocks into standalone asm if that better reflects the target object layout; preserve the C file as a declaration/source anchor when needed.
naming_conventions: Address-derived item symbols remain in the `it_802xxxxx`/`Item_802xxxxx` style, e.g. `Item_80267130`, `it_8026B1D4`, and `it_802C0DBC`.
New item inline header uses guard `MELEE_IT_INLINES_H` and a helper named `GetItemData` plus macro `GET_ITEM`.
`it_26B1.h` switched to an address-style include guard `GALE01_26B1D4`.
Several item headers use normalized `MELEE_IT_*_H` guards, shown by `itPKFlash.h` changing from `_itPKFlash_h_` to `MELEE_IT_ITPKFLASH_H`.
Forward typedefs use canonical struct names without the old underscore tag style where possible, e.g. `typedef struct CollData CollData;` and `struct CollData { ... };`.
The code keeps subsystem prefixes in type names: `Item_GObj`, `Fighter_GObj`, `ItemStateTable`, `ItemStateDesc`, `ItemLogicTable`, `itSword_ItemVars`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted separate asm files for `it_3F14.s`, `it_3F23.s`, and `it_3F31.s` and removed their object entries, then assigned the combined data range to `it_2725.c`.
Added `.file "it_2725.c"`, `.file "itcapsule.c"`, `.file "itparasol.c"`, and `.file "itsword.c"` directives to generated/standalone asm files to preserve source identity.
Changed `obj_files.mk` so `itcapsule`, `itparasol`, and `itsword` use asm object outputs in the link, despite having C source files in the tree.
Reclassified object states in `configure.py`: `item.c` and `it_26B1.c` to `Matching`; several individual item files to `Matching`; others to `NonMatching` rather than `Unknown`.
Moved internal prototypes to the top of `item.c` with explicit address comments, which helps control symbol order and static linkage during matching.
Reduced C files such as `itsword.c`, `itparasol.c`, and `itcapsule.c` while adding corresponding asm bodies, indicating a tactical fallback to asm for nonmatching regions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in this PR slice.
Because there was no human discussion excerpt, reviewer intent and detailed rationale cannot be established beyond the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Check the full diff around `GALE01/splits.txt` to confirm all old `it_3F14`, `it_3F23`, and `it_3F31` data ranges were intentionally folded into `it_2725.c`.
Inspect later PRs touching `src/melee/it/inlines.h` to see whether `GET_ITEM` or `GetItemData` became the preferred convention.
Look for subsequent relink PRs that use the same pattern of `.dox` sidecars plus forward header cleanup.
Audit future changes to `itcapsule.c`, `itparasol.c`, and `itsword.c` for when their asm fallbacks are replaced by matching C.
Search for more PRs that reclassify `Unknown` objects to `Matching`/`NonMatching` in `configure.py` after link-layout fixes.
